objective: Implement product-wide observability and statistics V2 for the whole VK AI Aggregator product: VK Bot, VK Mini App, jobs, workers, providers, payments, billing, referrals, admin/operator APIs, provider webhooks, artifacts and future website readiness.

non_goals: Do not run live YooKassa money actions without separate explicit confirmation.
Do not run real provider-spend synthetics without separate explicit confirmation.
Do not expose Grafana, Prometheus, Loki, Tempo, Alertmanager, OTel, exporters or /metrics publicly.
Do not introduce frontend-side billing, provider calls or trusted identity logic.

constraints.text: Do not log, metric-label, trace, alert, report or dashboard secrets, tokens, auth headers, full VK launch params, prompts, raw PII, raw provider payloads, raw payment payloads, private artifact URLs, raw URLs or idempotency keys.
VK Bot and VK Mini App must not call AI providers directly; provider calls must stay in worker/provider adapters.
Billing must remain ledger-based; payment top-ups must be granted only after provider-confirmed success through webhook/reconciliation flow.
External inbound events, payment webhooks, VK callbacks and job creation paths must remain idempotent.
Metrics and observability services must remain private: localhost, private Docker network, VPN or SSH tunnel only.
Prometheus labels must stay bounded. Forbidden labels include user_id, vk_user_id, job_id, payment_id, artifact_id, referral_code, idempotency_key, raw_error, raw_url, prompt and launch params.
Prefer cheap counters/histograms, recording rules and scheduled aggregate jobs. Avoid expensive DB queries on dashboard refresh and avoid per-request external billing/provider API calls.

rules: Per-request counters with bounded labels are low load.
Histograms are acceptable only with bounded labels and stable buckets.
Exact retention/cohort analytics must use scheduled aggregate tables or reports, not high-cardinality Prometheus labels.
Synthetic checks must run on low frequency and use mock/non-billable paths unless explicitly approved.
Real provider billing API polling must be scheduled/cached and never run per user request or per job.
Tracing must use sampling and must never include raw prompts, launch params or payload bodies.

checks: JSON validation for changed .agents, dashboards and report files
go test for touched packages; go test ./... before commit/push
npm --prefix web/miniapp audit --audit-level=high when frontend deps are relevant
frontend lint/typecheck/build when frontend is touched
promtool check config/rules when Prometheus config or rules are touched
blackbox_exporter --config.check when blackbox config is touched
docker compose config --quiet when compose files are touched
manual_reviews: Re-check provider boundary after every backend metrics/logging change.
Re-check billing ledger boundary after payment/statistics changes.
Re-check privacy and cardinality after every new metric, log, trace, alert, report or dashboard.
Record skipped checks with concrete reasons.

rules: New product metrics must use bounded enum/template labels only.
Route labels must be templates or allowlisted names, never browser raw URLs.
Exact unique-user and cohort analytics belong in scheduled aggregate tables or report jobs, not Prometheus labels.
Trace error status must use sanitized error_class, not raw err.Error().
Alert annotations must tell operators what to inspect and what not to paste into chat.

coverage_confirmed: VK Bot product funnel events and prompt-length buckets exist on inbound command/job paths.
Mini App BFF and frontend telemetry have safe client events, route templates, launch/payment/API/UI error metrics and prompt-length buckets.
Job orchestrator and worker expose job create, active-user event, terminal status, duration, retry, DLQ, queue depth and oldest-age metrics.
Provider adapters/worker path expose provider requests, latency, errors, rate limits, fallback, circuit state, tokens/media outputs and estimated cost.
Payments/billing expose payment intents, webhooks, provider errors, top-ups, payment-to-ledger latency, ledger entries, reservations, captures, releases and reconciliation mismatch metrics.
Referral service exposes aggregate registered/activated/rewarded/error outcomes without referral codes or user identifiers.
Admin/operator APIs now expose bounded admin action outcomes and auth failures.
Public metrics exposure checks cover app, vk and future website domains.
implemented_changes: Extended daily report queries across frontend, jobs, providers, payments, billing, queues, worker, VK delivery, referrals, auth/signature, admin actions and suspicious events.
Added weekly product report query spec.
Added provider/worker/job/payment-to-ledger recording rules and actionable Russian alert annotations for new V2 alerts.
Extended product synthetics runner with optional public metrics negative checks and explicit skipped safe-state records for protected mutating flows.
Sanitized tracing RecordError so Tempo receives bounded error_class instead of raw err.Error().
Added trace-aware JSON slog handler for safe trace_id/span_id correlation in runtime binaries.

skipped_or_deferred.reason: requires separate explicit confirmation because it can touch live payment/provider state
requires separate explicit confirmation because it can spend provider quota/money
should be implemented later through scheduled aggregate tables/event warehouse to avoid high-cardinality Prometheus labels

phases.objective: Map current observability coverage and identify gaps across all product surfaces before implementation.

tasks.text: Inventory current metrics, dashboards, alert rules, logs and traces for VK Bot, Mini App, jobs, workers, providers, payments, billing, referrals, artifacts and admin/operator APIs.
Audit existing metric labels, structured logs, traces, alert annotations and dashboards for high-cardinality fields or sensitive data.
Define a bounded V2 metric contract: allowed labels, forbidden labels, naming conventions, units and owner surface.
Update this plan with discovered gaps, implementation notes and any skipped checks.

phases.objective: Add privacy-safe funnel visibility for end-to-end product journeys across VK Bot and Mini App.

tasks.text: Track Mini App funnel steps: app_open/client_event, auth_ok, job_created, queued, provider_started, provider_succeeded, artifact_ready, artifact_viewed, payment_started and payment_succeeded.
Track VK Bot funnel steps: callback_received, dedup_accepted, command_parsed, control_response_sent, job_created, worker_processed, delivery_succeeded and delivery_failed.
Track job lifecycle transitions by surface, operation, modality and result without user/job IDs in labels.
Add or extend Grafana dashboard panels for cross-surface user journey funnels and drop-off rates.

phases.objective: Make product health actively tested and alerts actionable in Russian without leaking sensitive data.

tasks.text: Implement or refine synthetic checks for API health, provider-webhook health, Mini App BFF safe path, VK bot mock callback path, mock/non-billable job path and artifact access path.
Keep and extend public exposure checks for /metrics and observability endpoints on app/vk/future website domains.
Ensure alerts include Russian annotations: what happened, impact, where to go, what to check, probable causes, safe chat output and forbidden data.
Verify Telegram alert payloads never include secrets, raw ids, prompts, launch params, raw payloads or private artifact URLs.

phases.objective: Add low-load business/product statistics for the whole product.

tasks.text: Implement active user metrics where active means created at least one job, including chat text jobs and image/video jobs. Prefer scheduled aggregates; never label by user_id.
Track text/image/video usage, jobs per user/day, success rate and first job success rate with bounded labels.
Track payment funnel: intent_created, confirmation_opened when safe, webhook_accepted, provider_verified_success, ledger_topup_posted and repeat_job_after_payment.
Track referral registered, activated and rewarded aggregate counts without exposing referral codes, VK user IDs or internal UUIDs.
Create daily/weekly Telegram report queries for product usage, job success, payments, provider errors and security alerts.

phases.objective: Make provider reliability and economics visible without per-request external polling.

tasks.text: Track provider/model success rate, error_class rate, rate_limit events, fallback events, circuit breaker state and p95 latency.
Track estimated spend and charged credits by provider/model/operation. Add real provider billing import only as scheduled/cached future-safe design.
Add Grafana panels for provider reliability, cost, fallback and margin-readiness.

phases.objective: Improve visibility into async processing, stuck jobs, retry pressure and DLQ health.

tasks.text: Track queue depth, oldest queued job age, worker throughput and per-operation processing rate.
Track jobs stuck by status, retry counts, retry reasons, DLQ count and DLQ reasons with bounded error_class labels.
Add/extend worker dashboard for queue pressure, retry pressure, stuck jobs and delivery failures.

phases.objective: Improve trace/log correlation across the product without leaking sensitive data.

tasks.text: Extend trace coverage across inbound, auth, job orchestrator, outbox, worker, provider, artifact and delivery paths with sampling.
Standardize safe structured log fields: trace_id, correlation_id, surface, operation, modality, provider, status and error_class.
Audit logs for forbidden fields and add tests/helpers where practical to prevent raw payload or prompt logging.

phases.objective: Make observability useful for operators and product/business review.

tasks.text: Add Product Executive Overview dashboard for availability, active users, jobs, payments, provider health and active incidents.
Add User Journeys dashboard for Mini App, VK Bot, payments and referrals.
Review Security Config dashboard for public exposure, unsafe config and alert coverage.
Update runbook/docs only for material behavior, startup, alert or dashboard changes. Do not create redundant human-readable plans.
tasks.skipped_reason: No separate human-readable runbook update was required; behavior and operational notes are captured in machine-readable plan/report/rule artifacts.

definition_of_done.text: Observability V2 covers VK Bot, VK Mini App, jobs, workers, providers, payments, billing, referrals, artifacts, admin/operator APIs and future website readiness.
No observability endpoint or /metrics endpoint is public; public exposure checks and alerts are present.
No secrets, prompts, full launch params, raw PII, raw URLs, private artifact URLs or raw provider/payment payloads appear in metrics, logs, traces, alerts, dashboards or reports.
Prometheus label cardinality is bounded and explicitly reviewed.
Critical and warning alerts are actionable in Russian and explain what to inspect without leaking sensitive data.
Relevant checks are green or explicitly documented as skipped with reasons.
This plan's phase/task statuses and notes are updated during implementation and set to complete only after implementation and checks are done.
